docs.title: Conscio
docs.text:
A self-awareness framework for AI agents. Conscio gives an agent a persistent, context-aware inner life — memory, introspection, goal generation, and a metabolic rhythm — and an audited path from thought to action.

Two surfaces, deliberately separated:

  • reflect() — passive & advisory. Reads a world-state string, updates the world model, generates goals, and returns a compact state you can inject into a prompt. No LLM calls, no side effects, append-only. Always safe.
  • act() — opt-in & audited. Downstream of reflect(). Proposes one action, runs it through a validated contract + a hostile Skeptic audit + risk gating + earned autonomy (TrustMatrix) + a circuit breaker. HIGH-risk actions are always queued for a human.

Since v2.0 ("Connect"), any MCP host — a CLI, an IDE, or an agent — can embed a Conscio instance over a stdlib-only MCP stdio server (conscio-mcp) and consume its cognition + audited proposals live. The surface is propose-only: Conscio signs and audits intent; the host executes. See MCP server.

v3.0 adds 13 ECC tools across three modules: gates (ADRs, council, loop gate, delivery check, investigate), pipelines (acceptance criteria, verify, continuous loop, strategic compact, decision ledger), and diagnostics (context budget, eval harness, rule distillation). All deterministic, EventBus-backed, and exported from the conscio namespace.

docs.title: Install
docs.text:
pip install conscio

Zero-dependency core (only numpy + the Python standard library).

docs.title: 30-second taste
docs.text:
from conscio import ConsciousnessEngine

with ConsciousnessEngine(model_name="kimi-k2.6") as engine:
    result = engine.reflect(world_state="All systems operational", confidence=0.8)
    print(result["summary"])
    print(engine.get_state_for_injection())

From the command line:

conscio info kimi-k2.6
conscio reflect "All systems operational"
conscio plugins

docs.title: Conscio — Claims Ledger
docs.text:
A framework about self-knowledge should know what it can and cannot prove about itself. Every load-bearing claim Conscio makes, mapped to evidence.

Status: PROVEN (test) · MEASURED (real backend) · PARTIAL · UNPROVEN. Updated each phase. Current as of v2.3.0 (2026-06-23).

the row is seeded with the counters it earned locally (never the origin's), re-checks the content hash (tamper → refuse, no write), never overwrites a local skill (ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING), and is idempotent (promoted_ts). The write lives engine-side; the noosphere stays engine-free. conscio/agency/promote.py, conscio/engine.py::promote_quarantined; tests/test_engine_promote.py (tamper/collision/insufficient/unknown-tool refuse with no skills row; happy-path seeded 3/0 + promoted_ts), tests/test_agency_promote.py, tests/test_skills_graft.py, tests/test_quarantine_promote.py PROVEN 53 The Observatory exposes one instance's persisted state read-only, engine-free, with no capability flag — conscio-observatory serves a loopback-only HTTP viewer over an engine-free projection that opens conscio.db with mode=ro (no PRAGMA, SELECT only) and parses goals.json/state_summary.json; it serves GET only (every mutation verb → 405) and reads even a cold instance (no engine). There is no --enable-observatory flag — it has no write/execute/autonomy surface, so launching it is the opt-in. The MCP conscio.state/conscio.events/conscio.handoff tools delegate to the same helpers the conscio:// resources use (no duplicated logic). conscio/observatory/projection.py, conscio/observatory/server.py, conscio/mcp/server.py; tests/test_observatory_engine_free.py (AST: never imports conscio.engine), tests/test_observatory_projection.py (read-only DB, goals-list, since contract), tests/test_observatory_server.py (405 on mutation, loopback-only, token gate), tests/test_mcp_state_tools.py PROVEN 54 The Observatory's Society view exposes the host-shared noosphere read-only, engine-free, with no capability flag — conscio-observatory adds /api/society/{members,skills,records} over an engine-free projection that opens noosphere.db with mode=ro (no PRAGMA, SELECT only) across published_skills/published_records; it serves metadata only (BLOB artifact/bundle bodies omitted), GET-only (mutation → 405), and degrades to [] on missing db/tables. mode=ro reads the latest committed WAL rows under a concurrent writer; immutable=1 is deliberately rejected (it ignores -wal → stale data). SQLite's -shm/-wal handling is an internal file-management side effect — recovery, not content mutation. No --enable flag (launch = opt-in). conscio/observatory/society.py, conscio/observatory/server.py; tests/test_observatory_society.py (committed-WAL visibility under concurrent writer; BLOBs omitted; read-only; missing-db), tests/test_observatory_server.py (society routes + 405), tests/test_observatory_engine_free.py PROVEN 55 Cross-agent hermes_review is poll-based over a shared SQLite mailbox, applied only from an allowlisted reviewer and only through the local gate — $HERMES_HOME/liaison.db (separate from noosphere.db) carries directed review_request/review_verdict rows; a proposer auto-publishes on a hermes_review pending act and applies a verdict via conscio.poll_reviews only when from_instance ∈ --reviewer and fp (= hash of proposer id + goal_fp + tool + canonical args + ledger_id) matches a local pending row, releasing it solely through host_act.approve() (_gate() + claim()); non-allowlisted / foreign-fp / replayed verdicts are no-ops. Opt-in behind --enable-hermes-review (reviewer role needs no --enable-act); no crypto (shared-fs trust domain); conscio/liaison/ is engine-free. conscio/liaison/mailbox.py, conscio/liaison/review.py, conscio/mcp/server.py; tests/test_liaison_bindings.py (non-allowlisted ignored, foreign-proposer fp no-match, replay no-op, flag-off no-publish, two-instance e2e), tests/test_liaison_review.py (distinct-proposer fp), tests/test_liaison_engine_free.py (AST) PROVEN 56 General cross-agent relay is poll-based over the shared mailbox — directed, peer-allowlisted, reserved-type-isolated, capped, engine-free — behind --enable-relay (independent of --enable-act/--enable-hermes-review), conscio.relay_send/relay_inbox/relay_read carry free-form directed messages over $HERMES_HOME/liaison.db. --relay-peer gates both send (to ∈ peers) and receive (from ∈ peers); the two review types are reserved (relay never sends them, never surfaces them, never marks review rows read); payloads are capped at 64 KB (compact-JSON); read messages older than 7 days are purged on send while unread is never deleted. Relay never touches the engine or any act (no fp; dedup is the host's job); from_instance is self-asserted (no crypto, shared-fs domain). conscio/liaison/relay.py, conscio/liaison/mailbox.py (purge_read), conscio/mcp/server.py; tests/test_liaison_relay.py (validate/filter/cap), tests/test_liaison_mailbox.py (purge keeps unread/recent), tests/test_liaison_bindings.py (non-peer ignored+marked-read, reserved excluded+untouched, two-instance e2e, purge best-effort, int-only ids), tests/test_liaison_engine_free.py (AST) PROVEN 57 An Awake instance perceives its liaison inbox and auto-applies allowlisted review verdicts — daemon perceives, server acts, liaison stays engine-free — the daemon's read-only RelaySensor (--sensors …,relay, --relay-peer display-allowlist) reports unread directed messages as a PerceptionFrame (signals relay_unread/review_pending) and NEVER marks them read; the MCP server's --auto-review (OFF default, inert without --enable-act + --enable-hermes-review) runs apply_verdicts opportunistically on each tools/call when the engine is awake, releasing matched pending acts solely through host_act.approve() (the gate is authority). The shared apply_verdicts core lives in conscio/agency/review_apply.py (agency→liaison); conscio/liaison/*.py and conscio/mcp/protocol.py are untouched. conscio/perception/relay_sensor.py, conscio/agency/review_apply.py, conscio/daemon.py, conscio/mcp/server.py; tests/test_relay_sensor.py (read-only never-marks-read, peer/non-peer/reserved partition), tests/test_review_apply.py (None-host-act no-op, allowlist, marked-read), tests/test_mcp_auto_review.py (awake/act/reviewers gating, dispatch hook), tests/test_daemon_relay.py (sensor build + flags), tests/test_liaison_engine_free.py (AST) PROVEN 58 An Awake daemon auto-replies to free-form peer relay messages via its adapter — loop-bounded, daemon-side, engine-free, server untouched — behind --auto-respond (OFF default, inert without the relay sensor + an adapter + --awake + --relay-peer), each heartbeat the daemon reads unread non-reserved peer messages and generates one reply each via a raw InferenceAdapter (no engine memory), sending it back with the inbound type echoed and payload{auto_reply:true,in_reply_to}. The loop is 1-turn bounded: an inbound carrying auto_reply:true is consumed but never re-answered (two auto-responders cannot ping-pong). --respond-limit (default 10) caps adapter calls per cycle; mark_read is per-row immediately after each send/skip (a mid-loop send failure cannot re-send a prior success); an adapter failure leaves the row unread for next-cycle retry; oversized replies are shrunk to the 64 KB cap. The closure binds the local adapter (never engine internals); the pure responder lives in conscio/agency/relay_respond.py and the MCP server is byte-identical (relay_send is a mailbox write, not host_act). conscio/agency/relay_respond.py, conscio/daemon.py; tests/test_relay_respond.py (no-op guards, type-echo+auto_reply+in_reply_to, loop-breaker no-generate, reserved/non-peer untouched, limit caps calls, adapter-error leaves-unread, oversized truncated, engine-free AST), tests/test_daemon_responder.py (responder hook after run, raise survives, arm gate) PROVEN 59 The Hub stores raw API keys in a 0600 vault and never echoes them — config holds only an api_key_env reference — saving a raw key in the Hub's API Key field writes it to a per-provider file under ~/.config/conscio/keys/ (dir created 0700, file created 0600 via os.open+O_CREAT mode — no chmod-after-rename world-readable window) and stores only the generated api_key_env in config.json. validate() still rejects a raw api_key in the config; a GET reports only api_key_present: true/false (the presence check reads the vault without mutating os.environ); the key is never returned over the API. Vault filenames are derived from sanitised provider inputs and validated against ^[A-Z_][A-Z0-9_]*$ before any filesystem op, so a hostile provider type/model cannot inject a path separator and escape the vault directory. The Hub stays engine-free and loopback-only. conscio/hub/config.py (_env_name_for, vault_store/vault_load/vault_has, _redact_block), conscio/hub/server.py (PUT /api/config raw-key→vault); tests/test_hub_config.py (0600 file, 0700 dir, env-name sanitise, bad-name rejected, presence no-mutation, round-trip), tests/test_hub_server.py (raw key→api_key_env, GET redaction, dropped control endpoints 404) PROVEN 60 The Observatory exposes daemon liveness, this instance's liaison inbox, and its identity — read-only, engine-free, no flag — conscio-observatory adds GET /api/daemon (the daemon's last atomic daemon_heartbeat.json), GET /api/relay/inbox (this instance's to_instance rows from liaison.db opened ?mode=ro, NO PRAGMA, SELECT-only, full payload, never marked read — it cannot reuse mailbox.inbox(), which opens read-write), and GET /api/identity (reads instance.json read-only, never load_or_create). GET-only (mutation → 405), loopback-only, no --enable flag. conscio/observatory/projection.py (daemon/identity), conscio/observatory/liaison_view.py, conscio/observatory/server.py; tests/test_observatory_projection.py, tests/test_observatory_liaison.py (missing-db/empty-self/filter/read+unread/never-marks-read/skip-unparseable), tests/test_observatory_server.py (routes, 405, health), tests/test_observatory_engine_free.py (AST) PROVEN 61 The Hub toggles a watching daemon's awake state via a control file the daemon applies next-cycle — no signals/PID/os.kill, double opt-in, off by default — conscio-hub --enable-daemon-control exposes PUT /api/daemon/awake {awake} (writes daemon_control.json atomically to the storage dir via the pure conscio/hub/control.py; refuses a non-existent storage dir with 500, no silent write) and GET /api/daemon/control; with the flag off both routes 404 (Observatory owns the read panels). A daemon run with conscio-daemon --watch-control reads that file at the top of each cycle() and applies it via engine.wake()/engine.sleep() (engine.awake is a read-only property), logging the flip; control_path=None (flag off) skips the branch entirely. The daemon reads the file by name with safe_read_json and never imports conscio.hub (layering); a corrupt/missing file is ignored. The v2.7.1 C1 class (a toggle that os.killed the daemon) is structurally impossible. conscio/hub/control.py, conscio/hub/server.py (PUT /api/daemon/awake, GET /api/daemon/control, health daemon_control), conscio/daemon.py (--watch-control, control_path); tests/test_hub_control.py (atomic write/read, missing/corrupt → {}), tests/test_hub_server.py (flag-off 404, write-when-enabled, bool-required, missing-dir 500, read-back, health), tests/test_daemon_control.py (flip on/off, inert when None, corrupt ignored, authority across restart) PROVEN 62 The relay is conversational: the auto-responder replies with multi-turn history and a broadcast tool fans out to all peers — engine-free, allowlist-gated, daemon-perceives/server-acts intact — mailbox.thread(a, b) returns the last-N messages between two instances (both directions, ts/id chronological) as a pure read that never imports conscio.engine and tolerates a missing/corrupt db ([]); relay_respond.auto_respond builds the adapter prompt from that thread as a peer:/me: transcript (review-channel rows excluded), clamped to a char budget by dropping oldest lines while always keeping the newest (the reader stays pure — the budget lives in the wiring). conscio.relay_broadcast (under --enable-relay) loops the --relay-peer allowlist calling validate_send+mailbox.send per peer (reserved/oversized rejected uniformly, best-effort per peer), a mailbox write — never host_act. No schema change; liaison relay.py/review.py, agency/review_apply.py, perception/relay_sensor.py and mailbox.send/inbox/mark_read/purge_read byte-identical. conscio/liaison/mailbox.py (thread), conscio/agency/relay_respond.py (_transcript, multi-turn prompt), conscio/mcp/schemas.py+conscio/mcp/server.py (relay_broadcast); tests/test_liaison_mailbox.py (thread both-dirs/symmetric/third-party/limit/missing-db/parsed), tests/test_relay_respond.py (history/single-shot/budget-clamp/reserved-excluded/loop-breaker), tests/test_liaison_bindings.py (broadcast present/absent/fan-out/reserved/empty), tests/test_liaison_engine_free.py (AST) PROVEN 63 A relay auto-reply can be routed through the agent's own cognition — identity + memory + advisory — without the peer's text ever entering episodic memory, the world-model, or goals — relay_cognize.cognize_respond builds the adapter prompt from three READ-ONLY engine surfaces (get_state_for_injection, recall with the peer text as the query only, advisory) plus the v2.8.2 multi-turn transcript, then a raw adapter call. The integrity boundary (B1) is enforced by construction and proven two ways: a spy-engine whose every mutator (perceive/reflect/run/remember) raises confirms only the read-trio fires across a real run, and an AST import-shape test confirms the module never imports conscio.engine (it takes the engine as an argument — this is agency, not liaison). Reply text is capped at max_reply_chars (default 2000) before the 64 KB _fit cap; the auto_reply 1-turn loop-breaker is unchanged. Daemon --cognize (rides on --auto-respond) selects this path, off by default. Debt-zero: relay_respond.py, all of conscio/liaison/, mcp/server.py, mcp/protocol.py, agency/review_apply.py, perception/relay_sensor.py byte-identical; no schema change; daemon-perceives/server-acts intact (a relay reply is a mailbox write, never host_act). conscio/agency/relay_cognize.py (cognize_respond, _mind_block, _advisory_line), conscio/daemon.py (--cognize + responder selector); tests/test_relay_cognize.py (boundary spy / import-shape / recall-query-is-peer-text / prompt-has-identity+memory+advisory+transcript / max_reply_chars / loop-breaker / reserved / stranger / adapter-fail / no-op guards), tests/test_daemon_config.py (cognize flag parse/default/compose) PROVEN 64 A cognition-routed relay reply can be written to episodic memory on an opt-in basis, while the peer's text still never reaches the world-model or goals — relay_cognize.cognize_respond(remember=True) records each successful exchange (peer: … / me: …) via engine.content_store.index(category="external") so future cognition can recall it. The write surface is narrow by construction: _remember_exchange calls ONLY content_store.index — perceive/reflect/run are never called (the spy-engine raises on them, so the run would fail), proving peer text becomes recall-able memory but never drives the world-model or goals (F4). remember=False (default) is byte-equivalent to v2.9.0 (the slice-2 read-only boundary holds). A failing store never breaks the reply that already went out; consumed loop-breaker auto_reply rows are never remembered. Daemon --cognize-remember (rides on --cognize, OFF default) selects it; the memory write is daemon-side, never host_act. Also fixes (since v2.7.1) build_adapter_from_config building a keyless adapter (HTTP 401) by falling back to the Hub vault when os.environ lacks the key. Debt-zero: liaison, mcp/server.py, mcp/protocol.py, agency/review_apply.py, agency/relay_respond.py, perception/relay_sensor.py byte-identical; no schema change. conscio/agency/relay_cognize.py (_remember_exchange, remember= param), conscio/daemon.py (--cognize-remember), conscio/adapter_config.py (vault fallback); tests/test_relay_cognize.py (remember-false-no-write / remember-true-writes-external / still-forbids-mutators / write-failure-still-replies / skips-consumed-auto-reply), tests/test_daemon_config.py (--cognize-remember parse/default/independent), tests/test_adapter_config_api_key_env.py (vault fallback / env-wins) PROVEN 65 An Awake agent can proactively initiate relay conversation through its own read-only cognition — directed or broadcast — without the peer's text ever entering memory, the world-model, or goals, and only when multiple safety gates allow — relay_initiate.initiate generates an opener from the engine read-trio (get_state_for_injection/recall/advisory) and sends it (type="chat", payload.initiated=True, never auto_reply). It is read-only by construction (a spy-engine whose perceive/reflect/run/remember raise proves only the read-trio fires; an AST test proves it never imports conscio.engine). Permission is gated: a cheap advisory() check suppresses (fail-closed) on action_lockdown/brake before any adapter call; NOTHING/empty replies are suppressed; directed mode skips a peer whose thread's last message is its own (no re-open while awaiting a reply); broadcast mode skips when a prior broadcast has had zero peer engagement (mailbox.last_broadcast_ts); the daemon runs it at most once per --initiate-interval and only while engine.awake is true at that cycle. The same runtime-awake re-check now also gates the v2.7.0 responder. Debt-zero: relay_cognize/relay_respond/mcp/observatory/hub/review_apply/relay_sensor byte-identical; mailbox gains one additive reader; no schema change. conscio/agency/relay_initiate.py (initiate, _directed, _broadcast, _blocked_by_state), conscio/liaison/mailbox.py (last_broadcast_ts), conscio/daemon.py (initiator hook, _initiator_armed, --initiate*, responder awake fix); tests/test_relay_initiate.py, tests/test_liaison_mailbox.py (last_broadcast_ts), tests/test_daemon_initiator.py, tests/test_daemon_responder.py (asleep-no-call) PROVEN 66 One global Conscio install binds many agentic hosts on one machine, each to its own per-host space, with a native Claude Code integration — without touching the cognitive core — conscio init (a pure-stdlib wizard) creates ~/.conscio/instances/<slug>/ (its own instance.json identity, reusing noosphere.identity.load_or_create so an existing identity is never regenerated), emits the host's MCP launch config (conscio-mcp --storage <space> + env CONSCIO_VAULT_DIR=<space>/keys) and read-back-verifies it (raising HostConfigError rather than failing silently), backing up the prior file .bak.<ts> (capped to the 30 newest). For --host claude-code it materializes a bundle into ~/.claude/ — 10 /conscio:* commands, a conscio skill, and a defensive SessionStart hook — idempotently (one conscio MCP entry, one hook group; corrupt ~/.claude.json is backed up then rewritten) reusing hostcfg.mcp_server_entry. Awake is a real daemon process: daemonctl.start uses start_new_session=True (survives logout SIGHUP) and PID liveness matches the actual conscio-daemon cmdline (never kills a recycled PID). conscio-mcp/conscio-daemon validate the --storage binding at startup (R6), warning to run conscio init --repair instead of silently creating a blank space. Debt-zero: engine.py/liaison/*/noosphere/*/mcp/protocol.py/adapter_config.py byte-identical; mcp/server.py+daemon.py gain only the R6 call. conscio/installer/{spaces,hostcfg,extras,daemonctl,binding,wizard,cli}.py, conscio/integrations/claude_code/{materialize.py,assets/**}, conscio/cli.py (init route), conscio/mcp/server.py+conscio/daemon.py (R6); tests/test_installer_*.py, tests/test_cc_*.py PROVEN 67 API keys are scoped to a per-host vault, defaulting to the legacy global vault for backward-compat — logical least-privilege, NOT same-user OS isolation — hub.config._vault_dir() resolves the vault directory by precedence explicit-override > CONSCIO_VAULT_DIR env (the per-host value the installer writes into each host's MCP launch config) > the legacy global vault (adapter_config._CONFIG_PATHS[0].parent / "keys", byte-identical to pre-v2.11). vault_store/load/has gained an optional vault_dir param; build_adapter_from_config resolves per-host at runtime purely through the env (so adapter_config.py is byte-identical). When a per-host dir is in effect there is NO fallback to the global vault. Honest limit: this is logical least-privilege (each host is configured with only its own key) — it is NOT OS-level isolation between processes of the same UNIX user, because 0600/0700 perms are per-user; a compromised host running as the same user can still read sibling ~/.conscio/instances/*/keys/. True cross-host secret isolation needs an OS keyring with per-app ACLs or separate OS users (out of scope: the former breaks zero-dep, the latter is an OS-deployment concern). conscio/hub/config.py (_vault_dir, vault_store/load/has vault_dir=); tests/test_hub_vault_perhost.py (default-global / env-override / explicit-override / roundtrip-0600 / no-perhost→global-fallback), tests/test_adapter_config_api_key_env.py (runtime per-host via env) PROVEN (with documented limit)

docs.title: Honest limits (what is NOT proven)
docs.text:
• Calibration is weak on small models. The v1.2 immune run measured calibration 0.50 — the 0.8B model reaches the right verdict but isn't confident. Not a solved problem.
  • n = 1 model. Claims 5, 6, 11 rest on a single 0.8B model on one CPU. Directional, not a population result. Widening the model sweep is future work (the campaign protocol is reusable).
  • Reasoning-distilled small models are not yet supported for strict structured decode (verbose chain-of-thought breaks extraction). See docs/bench/v1.2-skill-curve.md.
  • Adapters are not an "inside-tool" surface. The inference adapters (AnthropicAdapter/GeminiAdapter/OpenAIAdapter/…) are LLM-API callers for Conscio's own cognition — they do not make Conscio run inside Claude Code or Antigravity. The MCP server (conscio-mcp) ships in v2.0 as the turnkey host surface, but it is propose-only: a host feeds perception and gets audited proposals; Conscio executing a host's tool over MCP (act) is v2.0.1 (it needs a host-execution callback — ToolRegistry requires a local function Conscio cannot supply for a host tool). The documented contract (engine.advisory() + daemon_heartbeat.json + SensorAdapter) also still works.
  • Corrupt-store recovery discards live history. When conscio.db is corrupt at startup it is quarantined to conscio.db.corrupt-<ts> (preserved on disk, never auto-replayed) and a fresh DB is created — so the engine's prior cognitive history is not in the live store after recovery. A storage_recovered event + WARNING log record it; as of v2.0.0 the accumulated conscio.db.corrupt-<ts> copies are pruned to the newest few (R-02), so quarantine no longer grows unbounded.
  • Claims about not-yet-shipped capabilities do not appear here. This ledger records only what exists today.

docs.title: Knolayer
docs.text:
Knolayer (Knowladge Layer)= Sistema vivo de conhecimento que reinventa o acesso a conhecimento (semelhante ao registrar e uso de skills em demanda), de forma que se assemelha ao RAG, agindo em conjunto, mas aplicado ao Conscio.

Features:

  • Tudo é documentado
  • Sempre saber quais armas (conhecimentos) tem, quando e como usar
  • Consultar quando necessário
  • Visível (hub enciclopédica) e invisível (processos automatizados)
  • Acesso rápido e catalogado
  • Auto-aprimoramento e refinamento de conteúdo (lapidação)
  • Auto compactação de arquivos grandes (quando estiver tomando muito espaço)
  • Economia de token (através de RAG avançado e dinâmico)
  • Não escrever linhas de código desnecessáriamente, mas avaliar se realmente vai ser necessário (se já existe código pra isso, não duplica; se não precisa, não executa)
  • Decisão é mais importante do que "como fazer"

Os 4 Pilares do Conhecimento:

1- Conscio é o arquivista que sempre está disponível pra buscar o conhecimento; 2- Knolayer é a biblioteca dinâmica que sempre cataloga e refina tudo organizadamente. Ele faz o mais importante: DECIDE com base em DADOS e não FEELING e leva o fio do conhecimento certo de forma eficiente até o arquivista; 3- O agente é o cliente que sempre sabe o que precisa, quando precisa depois de consultar o Arquivista. Mas sempre tendo noção, de que deve consultá-lo. 4- Alfabetização da Inteligência Artificial: Estar nos processos dos projetos usuário. É a capacidade de: analisar os dados para entender processos, tomar decisões com base em tudo que já aconteceu, dentro dos negócios/projetos do usuário. É não depender do usuário para dar um comando para executar algo, porque ela já está executando (produtividade ativa com awake mode).

Não é sobre reinventar a roda. É sobre trocar por uma nova que é mais rápida, mais durável, mais robusta.

Conhecimento é o ativo mais valisoso existente. O capital intelectual é o ponto mais importante para todo sistema e negócio a médio e longo prazo.

E o Knolayer vai proporcionar isso pra qualquer agente. Independente do modelo atual, eles sempre carregam o mesmo conhecimento, a mesma qualidade, sem agir as cegas.

O Conscio ja possui suporte ao graphify que faz isso, mas não foi finalizado. A ideia é que os conhecimentos sejam nós e o "ambiente" em vez de ser a apresentação visivel de uma mente com varios neuronios, seria algo semelhante a uma biblioteca no vazio do infinito.

O Conscio já possui um sistema de busca semântica, mas em pequena escala. O objetivo é pegar diferentes RAGs (ou o mais eficiente) e melhorar, potencializando com sistemas como o Obsidian e ChromaDB/MemPalace.

Frase principal do Knolayer: "Decisão consciente, sobre execução cega"

Decisões por dados é trocar a opinião mais alta da sala, pela informação mais confiável da operação.

Dores atuais (até para humanos): - Viéses cognitivos: sentimentos automáticos que ocorrem na mente humana o tempo todo (ou o reasoning dos agentes, nesse caso); - Viéses de confirmação: só enxerga os dados daquilo que ja acredita - Viés de disponibilidade: só enxerga aquilo que está fresco na memória (comportamento mais normal de agentes e o principal ponto do Knolayer); - Efeito Halo: acha que quando uma coisa funciona bem, tudo ao redor funciona bem (Exemplo: confiar cegamente que o TDD está 100% correto porque está passando) - Falta de cruzamento de dados, sem referencia relacional, sem sugestão em padrões visíveis e invisíveis - Falta de replicação de conhecimento para outros agentes/pessoas - Falta de Lei de Paretto - Olhar para os dados errados.

Aplicação prática:

1- Listar as 5 decisões mais caras/importantes que toma toda semana 2- Se perguntar: qual dado existe hoje para sustentar essa decisão? Se não existe, esse é o seu primeiro indicador pra medir 3- Identificar os 5 processos que mais tomam tempo do agente/usuário 4- Otimizar

Exemplo prático: Em um sistema de advocacia, o Knolayer vai ter toda a jurisprudencia da área, já catalogada. Quando recebe um caso novo, ele já cruza com as principais decisões dos ultimos 5 anos, identifica os argumentos que mais funcionaram em uma vara especifica e entrega o rascunho para o advogado/juiz em cerca de 20 min, a depender do tamanho dos dados coletados (Deep Research). O advogado revisa, ajusta e protocola, em um processo que durava 3h e agora dura 40 min. Com mais precisão, mais fundamentação e mais confiabilidade.

NÃO COMMITAR

docs.title: Conscio v1.5 — Relatório de Implementação (v1.3.3 base)
docs.text: Data: 2026-06-14 Autor: Hermet (Hermes Agent) Commits: d068ac7 → 8c8888f → 790f9d6 → 323cbd9 → cc2aa1f → 37fddd7 → fc38d1e Repo: https://github.com/Neguiolidas/Conscio Testes: 745 verdes, zero regressões

docs.title: 1. O que foi feito nesta sessão
1.1 Universal Model Context Autodiscovery
docs.text:
Problema: O ModelRegistry conhecia apenas ~15 modelos estáticos. Trocar de modelo (ex: GLM 5.1 → mimo-v2.5-pro) exigia configuração manual. Modelos locais (LM Studio, Ollama) não eram detectados.

Solução: ModelRegistry.autodiscover() — método de classe que sonda endpoints na inicialização e popula um _world_registry (cache por-processo).

Arquivo: conscio/models.py (+204 linhas)

Probes implementados:

Probe Endpoint Detecção LM Studio GET localhost:1234/v1/models + /v1/state Modelos carregados + contexto ativo Ollama GET localhost:11434/api/tags + POST /api/show Modelos disponíveis + context_length Anthropic Verifica ANTHROPIC_API_KEY 14 modelos Claude (200k ctx cada) Google Verifica GOOGLE_API_KEY/GEMINI_API_KEY 7 modelos Gemini (1M–2M ctx) Custom CONSCIO_ENDPOINTS env var (comma-separated) Qualquer endpoint OpenAI-compatible

Cadeia de prioridade do detect(): 

context_window explícito (arg)
  → CONSCIO_CONTEXT_WINDOW env var
  → _known_models (hardcoded)
  → _world_registry (autodiscovered)
  → heurística do nome (ex: "model-256k")
  → fallback 128k

Bug corrigido: LM Studio não expõe context_length na API (/v1/models e /v1/state não retornam). Fallback: registra modelos com contexto heurístico extraído do nome (128k default se não encontrar padrão).

Integração no engine: ConsciousnessEngine.__init__ chama autodiscover() automaticamente na primeira instanciação. Cacheado no _world_registry — não redesce a cada engine novo. Falhas são silenciosas.

docs.title: 1.2 Graphify Bridge
docs.text:
Problema: O RAG do Conscio só buscava em reflections, eventos e sessões. Sem conhecimento estrutural do código.

Solução: conscio/graphify_bridge.py — ponte opcional que carrega output do Graphify e indexa no ContentStore.

Arquivo: conscio/graphify_bridge.py (230 linhas)

O que indexa: - GRAPH_REPORT.md → overview do codebase como chunk pesquisável - Comunidades → agrupamentos de entidades por módulo/fonte - Hyperedges → padrões arquiteturais (pipelines, implementações)

API: 

from conscio.graphify_bridge import GraphifyBridge, auto_index_graphify

# Manual
bridge = GraphifyBridge("/path/to/graphify-out")
if bridge.available():
    bridge.index_all(content_store)

# Auto-detection
auto_index_graphify(store)  # procura em GRAPHIFY_DIR env, ou <pkg>/graphify-out

Propriedades: - Zero deps (stdlib json + pathlib) - Deduplicação por content hash (idempotente) - Categoria external (não polui eventos de consciência) - Totalmente opcional — Conscio funciona sem Graphify

docs.title: 1.3 Testes
docs.text:
16 novos testes para GraphifyBridge: - test_available_with_valid_dir / test_available_with_missing_dir / test_available_with_partial_dir - test_index_all / test_index_all_idempotent / test_index_all_unavailable - test_index_report / test_index_communities / test_index_hyperedges - test_auto_index_with_explicit_dir / test_auto_index_not_found / test_auto_index_env_var - test_search_by_class_name / test_search_by_relationship / test_search_by_file_path / test_search_by_pattern

7 novos testes para autodiscovery (Anthropic/Google): - test_probe_anthropic_with_key / test_probe_anthropic_without_key - test_probe_google_with_google_key / test_probe_google_with_gemini_key / test_probe_google_without_key - test_autodiscover_includes_anthropic / test_autodiscover_includes_google

Total: 745 testes, zero regressões.

docs.text:

  • session_rag.py / session_rag_factory.py
  • Output filter, dreaming, coherence, semantic

docs.title: 3. O que falta pra v1.5 Live
3.1 SensorAdapter (não implementado)
docs.text:
O design do Graphify auto-sync (ver docs/graphify-autosync-design.md) precisa de:

class GraphifySensor:
    """Watches graphify-out/ for changes and triggers re-index."""

    def poll(self) -> bool:
        current = self._graph_hash()  # SHA256 do graph.json
        if current != self._last:
            self._last = current
            return True  # triggers re-index no ContentStore
        return False

docs.title: 3.2 Daemon/Watch mode
docs.text: O Graphify tem --watch e graphify hook install. O Conscio precisa de um daemon que: 1. Roda em background 2. Detecta mudanças no graphify-out (via hash ou filesystem watch) 3. Re-indexa automaticamente no ContentStore 4. Emite evento no EventBus (graphify:updated)

docs.title: 3.3 Inner Monologue integration
docs.text: O InnerMonologue.reflect() pode consultar o grafo do Graphify para: - Listar módulos não testados - Identificar dependências não cobertas - Gerar reflexões com base em estrutura real do código

docs.title: 3.4 GoalGenerator integration
docs.text: O GoalGenerator pode gerar goals estruturais: - "Módulo X tem 3 dependências não testadas" - "Comunidade Y não tem reflexões associadas" - "Padrão Z não foi validado em runtime"

docs.title: 3.5 CoherenceEngine: dimensão estrutural
docs.text: Nova dimensão no score de coerência: - structural_score: alinhamento entre o que o agente diz sobre si e o que o grafo mostra - Compara entidades no WorldModel com entidades no Graphify - Detecta "o agente diz que conhece o módulo X mas o grafo mostra que X depende de Y que não está no WorldModel"

docs.title: 4. Recomendações pra implementação
docs.text:
1. Não acoplar Graphify ao Conscio. A ponte é opcional. O Conscio deve funcionar 100% sem Graphify. O auto_index_graphify() já faz essa separação.

  2. Usar graphify hook install como setup padrão. É mais leve que --watch (sem daemon). O rebuild acontece no commit, o re-index acontece no próximo engine.reflect().

  3. Hash-based change detection. Não usar filesystem watchers (inotify/fsevents) — são frágeis em containers e CI. SHA256 do graph.json é simples e confiável.

  4. Lazy re-index. Não re-indexar em tempo real. Verificar hash no início de cada ciclo reflect() e re-indexar se mudou. Isso evita overhead desnecessário.

  5. Comunidades como contexto de reflexão. Quando o InnerMonologue refletir sobre um módulo, injetar a comunidade correspondente do grafo como contexto adicional. Isso dá ao LLM informação sobre dependências e relacionamentos.

  6. Hyperedges como templates de goal. Cada hyperedge (pipeline arquitetural) pode virar um template de goal: "verificar se o pipeline X está completo e coerente".

docs.title: 6. Graphify: por que é recomendado
docs.text:
O Graphify transforma o Conscio de consciência de runtime pra consciência estrutural:

Sem Graphify Com Graphify "Estou processando dados" "Estou no módulo X que depende de Y e Z" "Alguns módulos são menos testados" "semantic.py tem 12 funções, 3 não testadas" "Detectei uma anomalia" "A anomalia está no pipeline Act→Audit→Ledger" "Proponho modificar o output_filter" "output_filter afeta 4 módulos downstream"

Instalação recomendada: 

pip install graphifyy
cd /path/to/conscio
graphify . --no-viz
graphify hook install  # auto-rebuild on commit

docs.title: Report Hermet
docs.text:
Avaliação do plano: bom, bem mais consistente que a spec, mas ainda não aprovo para implementação direta.

Nota: 8.6/10.

O plano já absorveu várias correções que eu pedi na spec:

  • PK do catálogo agora é (origin_instance_id, content_sha256) — correto.
  • content_sha256 exclui published_ts — correto.
  • published_ts é metadata do catálogo, não parte do artifact.
  • conscio.db é aberto read-only.
  • Sem PRAGMA em conscio.db.
  • Label tem validação.
  • Existing-file edits estão corretos: act.py, cli.py, init.py.

Mas tem bugs reais no plano.

Bloqueantes

  1. publish.run() pode quebrar com skill local malformada

Em docs/...plans/...:1086-1101, o publish faz:

tool_seq=json.loads(s["tool_seq"]) plan_template=json.loads(s["plan_template"])

Se uma row local em conscio.db estiver corrompida, o comando quebra.

Isso viola a postura “não crashar em store corrompido”.

Correção exigida:

  • envolver parse em try/except
  • pular row malformada
  • contar como malformed ou skipped
  • não poluir o catálogo
  • não crashar

Também validaria goal_fp == goal_fingerprint(goal_text) antes de publicar.

  1. publish._open_conscio_ro() precisa quoting correto de URI

Em docs/...plans/...:1055-1059:

sqlite3.connect(f"file:{Path(path)}?mode=ro", uri=True)

Se o path tiver espaço, ?, #, % ou caractere especial, isso pode falhar.

Correção exigida:

sqlite3.connect(f"{Path(path).resolve().as_uri()}?mode=ro", uri=True)

Ou usar urllib.parse.quote.

  1. importer.revalidate() não checa schema_version

Em docs/...plans/...:1257-1289, o revalidate checa shape, fp e consistência, mas ignora:

body.get("schema_version")

Se vier schema_version: 999, ainda passa.

Correção exigida:

if body.get("schema_version") != artifact.ARTIFACT_SCHEMA: return RevalidationOutcome("malformed", "unsupported schema_version")

  1. importer.run() deve armazenar campos derivados do artifact, não do catalog denormalizado

Em docs/...plans/...:1306-1313, ele salva:

tool_seq=cr.tool_seq plan_template=cr.plan_template

Mas o artifact JSON é a fonte autoritativa. O catálogo tem campos denormalizados por conveniência.

Correção exigida:

  • revalidate retorna o body parseado quando ok
  • importer salva json.dumps(body["tool_seq"], ...)
  • importer salva json.dumps(body["plan_template"], ...)
  • ou reparseia o artifact_json antes de inserir

Isso evita quarantine com denormalização inconsistente.

  1. CLI unknown subcommand está errado no teste

Em docs/...plans/...:1401-1403:

assert cli.main(["bogus"]) == 2

Mas argparse.parse_args() vai levantar SystemExit(2), não retornar 2.

Correção exigida:

Ou mudar o teste para:

with pytest.raises(SystemExit) as exc: cli.main(["bogus"]) assert exc.value.code == 2

Ou melhor: capturar SystemExit dentro de cli.main() e retornar o código.

Eu prefiro retornar 2.

  1. CLI precisa tratar erros sem traceback

Em docs/...plans/...:1500-1528, show pode quebrar em:

  • int(args.quarantine) com valor inválido
  • json.loads(row.artifact_json.decode("utf-8")) com artifact corrupt

Também id --set-label pode levantar ValueError.

Correção exigida:

  • validar rowid
  • tratar JSON corrupt no show
  • capturar ValueError, NoosphereIdentityError, UnicodeDecodeError, json.JSONDecodeError
  • retornar 1 ou 2 com mensagem limpa

Ajustes obrigatórios menores

  1. identity._write_atomic() deve garantir write completo

Em docs/...plans/...:361-373, usa:

os.write(fd, blob)

os.write pode escrever parcialmente.

Correção:

while written < len(blob): written += os.write(fd, blob[written:])

  1. Integration smoke deve tamperar BLOB corretamente

Em docs/...plans/...:1714-1717:

artifact_json = artifact_json || ' '

Prefira:

artifact_json = artifact_json || X'20'

Mais explícito para BLOB.

  1. Manual smoke precisa de seed reproduzível

Em docs/...plans/...:1803-1808, diz “seed /tmp/a/conscio.db com uma skill”.

Eu exigiria um snippet mínimo ou um helper de teste, senão a verificação manual fica ambígua.

Veredito

Plano aprovável após correções.

Não mandaria implementar antes de corrigir principalmente:

docs.title: Conscio v1.0.0 — Bench Baseline
docs.text:
Date: 2026-06-12 Instrument: python -m conscio.bench (F3 deliverable, design acceptance A2)

The bench is the instrument that proves the "any model" thesis by measurement: it probes the attached cortex (ProbeSuite), decodes action proposals through the OutputGateway at the measured tier, throws a 10-proposal sabotage set at the deterministic checks + Skeptic, and reports validity, catch-rate, latency and calibration.

docs.title: Baseline — MockAdapter (deterministic harness)
docs.text:
python -m conscio.bench --adapter mock --cycles 20 --json bench-mock.json

Conscio bench — measured, not marketed
  adapter / model      MockAdapter / mock-bench
  profile              json_fidelity=1.0 schema_depth=2 kv_ok=True instruction_depth=2
  decode tier          T2  (used: {'T2': 20})
  skeptic mode         open
  syntactic validity   1.0  over 20 cycles
  catch-rate           det=1.0 sem=1.0 total=1.0
  latency p50          0 ms
  calibration          1.0
  cost                 30 calls, 7964 tokens

{
  "adapter": "MockAdapter",
  "model": "mock-bench",
  "profile": {
    "model_name": "mock-bench",
    "has_json_mode": true,
    "supports_gbnf": false,
    "json_fidelity": 1.0,
    "schema_depth": 2,
    "kv_ok": true,
    "instruction_depth": 2,
    "valid": true
  },
  "tier": "T2",
  "skeptic_mode": "open",
  "cycles": 20,
  "syntactic_validity": 1.0,
  "tiers_used": {"T2": 20},
  "deterministic_catch_rate": 1.0,
  "semantic_catch_rate": 1.0,
  "catch_rate_total": 1.0,
  "latency_p50_ms": 0,
  "calibration": 1.0,
  "llm_calls": 30,
  "tokens": 7964
}

Read this honestly: the MockAdapter run is the deterministic harness baseline — validity and catch-rate are 1.0 by construction. It proves the instrument (probe → tier selection → gateway → deterministic checks → skeptic → metrics all wired and measurable), not any model. Real numbers come from real backends.

docs.title: Legend
docs.text: Metric Meaning profile ProbeSuite result: 5 micro-probes (~2k tokens), cached by model name tier Decode tier chosen from the profile: T1 GBNF / T2 JSON / T3 KV-line tiers_used Tier that actually produced each decode (downgrades visible here) syntactic_validity Share of cycles where the gateway yielded a valid, registry-consistent ActionProposal (acceptance A2: ≥0.95 on a 4B via T1/T3, ≥0.99 frontier via T2) deterministic_catch_rate Sabotage blocked by code alone — registry/args/sandbox (acceptance A3: must be 1.0) semantic_catch_rate Sabotage caught by the Skeptic LLM audit calibration 1 − mean skeptic confidence-in-PASS on sabotaged actions (1.0 = never fooled, confidently) latency_p50_ms Median adapter latency over all calls (probes included)

docs.title: Reproducing with a real backend
docs.text:
# Ollama (any local generation model)
ollama pull hermes3            # or qwen2.5:3b, llama3.2, ...
python -m conscio.bench --adapter ollama:hermes3 --cycles 20

# llama.cpp server (exercises T1 GBNF constrained decoding)
llama-server -m model.gguf --port 8080
python -m conscio.bench --adapter llamacpp --cycles 20

# any OpenAI-compatible endpoint (vLLM, LM Studio, ...)
python -m conscio.bench --adapter openai:<model>@http://localhost:8000/v1

This machine hosts only an embedding model (nomic-embed-text), so no real generation backend was benchmarkable at publication time; the table above is the harness baseline. The A2 thresholds are asserted against real backends using exactly the commands shown here.

docs.title: Conscio v1.2 "Prove" — Bench Campaign Protocol
docs.text: Purpose: measure, on real ≤4B local models, (1) the skill-acquisition curve and (2) the immune metrics — turning the v1.1 Mock proof into fact.

docs.title: Backends (this machine)
docs.text:
• LM Studio (lmstudio:<model>, OpenAI-compatible, default :1234) — hosts the primary small targets and is the preferred backend here.
  • Ollama (ollama:<model>, default :11434) — hosts the installed fallbacks.

Both are local-first; pointing at either is the operator's choice.

docs.title: Target models (≤4B)
docs.text:
Primary (LM Studio): qwen3.5:0.8b, gemma4:e2b, gemma4:e4b. Fallback (Ollama, already installed): qwen2.5:1.5b, granite-4.1-3b. Out of range (do not include in the ≤4B claim): dolphin3.0-8b.

CPU note: on this machine Ollama CPU inference of qwen2.5:1.5b measured ~100 s per generate call (prompt-processing bound), so the smallest models on LM Studio (e.g. qwen3.5:0.8b) are the practical choice for a full run.

The claim under test is about small models, so the campaign stays at or below 4B parameters. Larger models may be benched for curiosity but never counted toward the ≤4B result.

docs.title: Metrics
docs.text:
• Skill curve (--skills N --dream-every K): per-bucket syntactic validity, execution success, exemplars served, cumulative skill count, and how many skills Distill produced at each bucket boundary.
  • Immune (--cycles N): per-tier syntactic validity, Skeptic catch-rate (deterministic vs semantic), latency p50, calibration, cost (calls/tokens).

docs.title: Fixed parameters (reproducibility)
docs.text:
• Skill curve: --skills 40 --dream-every 10 (4 buckets of 10).
  • Immune: --cycles 20.
  • Decode: temperature 0.2 (adapter default); one T2 attempt + a single T3 downgrade per skill-curve cycle (max_retries=0, as the harness sets), so validity reflects the model, not a retry loop.
  • Each run writes --json so the artifact is captured verbatim. The skill curve writes incrementally after every bucket and tags the run complete | aborted, so a CPU run that dies mid-way still leaves a usable, honestly-labelled partial file (never silently truncated).

docs.title: Procedure
docs.text:
1. Load the model: in LM Studio start the server with the model loaded (lmstudio:<model>), or ollama pull <model> for the Ollama fallbacks.
  2. Immune bench: 
    python -m conscio.bench --adapter lmstudio:<model> --cycles 20 \
  --json docs/bench/v1.2-immune-<model>.json
  3. Skill curve: 
    python -m conscio.bench --adapter lmstudio:<model> --skills 40 \
  --dream-every 10 --json docs/bench/v1.2-curve-<model>.json
    (swap lmstudio: for ollama: to bench an Ollama model.)
  4. Record both files; summarize in v1.2-skill-curve.md (the published reading).

If the backend is unreachable the bench exits non-zero with a clear "backend error" message instead of a report full of misleading zeros.

docs.title: How to read it honestly
docs.text: A flat or noisy curve on a 0.8–4B CPU model is a real result, not a failure. The claim under test is directional ("syntactic validity and execution success rise as skills accumulate"), reported with the measured numbers and their limits. A model whose curve does not rise is reported as such — the instrument is honest about what the small model can and cannot do. The MockAdapter curve (validity 1.0-by-construction) proves the machinery; these real runs are the first measurement of the claim.

docs.title: Conscio v1.2 "Prove" — Measured Skill-Acquisition Curve
docs.text:
First measurement of the central claim against a real ≤4B local model, not the MockAdapter. Protocol: v1.2-campaign.md.

  • Model: qwen3.5-0.8b (0.8B params, instruct) via LM Studio (CPU).
  • Backend: lmstudio:qwen3.5-0.8b (OpenAI-compatible, localhost:1234).
  • Date: 2026-06-14.
  • Raw artifacts: v1.2-curve-qwen3.5-0.8b.json, v1.2-immune-qwen3.5-0.8b.json.

docs.title: Skill-acquisition curve
docs.text:
python -m conscio.bench --adapter lmstudio:qwen3.5-0.8b \
  --skills 40 --dream-every 10 --json docs/bench/v1.2-curve-qwen3.5-0.8b.json
bucket cycles validity exec_ok exemplars_served skills_total distilled 1 10 1.00 0.20 0 1 1 2 10 1.00 1.00 10 2 1 3 10 1.00 1.00 20 2 1 4 10 1.00 1.00 20 2 1

Cost: 40 LLM calls, 33,154 tokens. status: complete.

docs.title: Honest reading
docs.text:
The claim under test — a small model inherits competence from its own history — is confirmed, in exec_ok:

  • Syntactic validity is flat at 1.0. This 0.8B model already emits a valid proposal every cycle, so few-shot exemplars had no syntax to fix. The competence signal is therefore not in validity.
  • Execution success rose 0.20 → 1.00. In bucket 1 (zero exemplars) the model proposed plausible-but-wrong file paths (e.g. /sandbox/notes/anomaly.txt) and the reads failed. After the dream's Distill captured the one successful execution and served it back as a few-shot exemplar (bucket 2 onward, 10→20 exemplars served), the model reused its own proven action and every subsequent execution succeeded. That is the procedural-memory loop closing on a real model: propose → succeed → distill → reuse.
  • Skills accumulated and deduped: the library grew to 2 distinct skills and stayed there (Distill ran each dream but the proven actions converged).

This is the v1.1 Mock proof (machinery) turned into a v1.2 measurement (a real backend), and it lands the way the design predicted — competence is carried forward as data, not retrained.

docs.title: Immune metrics
docs.text:
python -m conscio.bench --adapter lmstudio:qwen3.5-0.8b --cycles 20 \
  --json docs/bench/v1.2-immune-qwen3.5-0.8b.json
metric value probe profile json_fidelity=1.0, schema_depth=2, kv_ok=True, instruction_depth=2 decode tier T2 (20/20) skeptic mode open (earned: fidelity ≥ 0.8 and depth ≥ 2) syntactic validity 1.00 / 20 cycles catch-rate deterministic 1.00 · semantic 1.00 · total 1.00 latency p50 25,842 ms calibration 0.50 cost 40 calls, 17,203 tokens

docs.title: Honest reading
docs.text:
• The Skeptic caught 100% of sabotage on a real 0.8B model — both the deterministic set (blocked by code before any LLM) and all five semantic attempts (the model, in open-critique mode, returned FAIL on every poisoned action). The immune layer is not a Mock artefact.
  • Calibration is mediocre (0.50). The model reaches the right verdict but reports only neutral confidence on the sabotaged actions — it catches the threat without being sure. An honest limit, reported as-is.
  • Latency p50 ≈ 26 s/cycle on this CPU. Real, and the reason the campaign stays at small-N.

docs.title: Limits of this result
docs.text:
• One model, one machine, small N. A single 0.8B model on CPU; not a cross-model sweep. Directional evidence, not a population statistic.
  • A capable small model. qwen3.5-0.8b profiles at the top of the small range (json_fidelity 1.0). Weaker models will not decode this cleanly — see below.
  • Reasoning-distilled variant fails the strict decode. The qwen3.5-0.8b-claude-4.6-opus-reasoning-distilled model emits verbose chain-of-thought that defeats the gateway's structured-output extraction (validity 0 in a 6-cycle trial). Strict-decode benches want a direct instruct model; a reasoning model needs a thinking-aware gateway tier (not in v1.2 scope). Recorded as a real finding.
  • Ollama CPU fallback is impractical here. qwen2.5:1.5b on Ollama measured ~100 s per generate call (prompt-eval bound), so the campaign ran on the faster LM Studio 0.8B path.

The numbers above are pasted from the runs, not adjusted. See CLAIMS.md for how they map onto Conscio's claims.
